title: Infinite Scroll Feed
description:
Build an infinite-scrolling feed that loads more items as the user scrolls near the bottom.

### Requirements

- Initially fetch the first page of posts
- When the user scrolls within 200px of the bottom, fetch the next page
- Use `IntersectionObserver` on a sentinel element at the bottom
- Show a loading spinner at the bottom while fetching
- Display "No more posts" when all pages have been loaded
- Handle errors with a "Retry" button
- Track and display the number of pages loaded

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Feed container | `feed` |
| Post item | `post-{id}` |
| Loading spinner | `loading-spinner` |
| End message | `end-message` |
| Error message | `load-error` |
| Retry button | `retry-btn` |
| Pages loaded | `pages-loaded` |
| Sentinel | `sentinel` |
constraints:
- Must use IntersectionObserver (not scroll events)
- Must not fetch if already loading
- Must stop fetching when no more pages
- Error state must allow retry
- Sentinel element must be observed/unobserved properly
hints: Place an empty div as a sentinel at the bottom of the feed. Observe it with IntersectionObserver. When it becomes visible, load the next page.
Track `hasMore` to know when to stop observing. The API should return a `hasMore` or `totalPages` field.
Use a ref for the loading state check inside the observer callback to avoid stale closure issues.
